content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 867 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 7007 • • • LOS ANGELES, 90012

August 17, 1981

Mr. Philip Bradford
914 South Washburn #5
Corona, California 91720

Dear Mr. Bradford:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you during our
August 11, 1981 telephone conversation.

As a new treasurer for a recipient committee, you asked how
to report contributions from spouses and whether or not the
Commission has issued anything in writing on this matter?

I advised you that based on an opinion issued by the Fair
Political Practices Commission on September 7, 1976, contributions
received from husband and wife are generally viewed to be from the
same source and are, therefore, to be reported as from the same
source. However, if the contributors can substantiate that husband
and wife have separate incomes and act independently of each other,
then the contributions may be reported as if from each independent
source.

I also advised that you, as the treasurer, are obligated to
verify this information by telephone or in writing for your records
in order to comply with the Political Reform Act. (Government
Code Section 84104.)

Enclosed is a copy of the Political Reform Act and the 1981
Recordkeeping Manual for your information and use. I hope this
information has been helpful to you, please do not hesitate to
contact me.

Sincerely,

Helen P. Arriola
Political Reform
Consultant

HPA:jg

Enclosures:

SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660 • LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.question: As a new treasurer for a recipient committee, how should contributions from spouses be reported and has the Commission issued anything in writing on this matter?
sections.conclusion: Based on an FPPC opinion from September 7, 1976, contributions from husband and wife are generally viewed as from the same source and must be reported as such. However, if contributors can substantiate that husband and wife have separate incomes and act independently, contributions may be reported from each independent source. The treasurer is obligated to verify this information by telephone or in writing for compliance with the Political Reform Act (Government Code Section 84104).
sections.question_synthetic: A new recipient committee treasurer asked how to report contributions from spouses and whether the FPPC has issued written guidance on this reporting requirement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Spousal contributions are generally reported as a single source unless the spouses can document separate incomes and independent action, and the treasurer must verify such information in writing or by phone.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Clear advice letter format with telephone conversation confirmation. Question and conclusion are implicit rather than explicitly labeled with headers. Minor OCR artifacts noted in document header but did not impede extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As a new treasurer for a recipient committee, how should contributions from spouses be reported and has the Commission issued anything in writing on this matter?

CONCLUSION: Based on an FPPC opinion from September 7, 1976, contributions from husband and wife are generally viewed as from the same source and must be reported as such. However, if contributors can substantiate that husband and wife have separate incomes and act independently, contributions may be reported from each independent source. The treasurer is obligated to verify this information by telephone or in writing for compliance with the Political Reform Act (Government Code Section 84104).

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter responds to a new recipient committee treasurer's question about reporting spousal contributions, confirming that such contributions are treated as one source unless the spouses can prove independent incomes and decision-making.
